meta.note: 80-150 word answers in Jamie's voice. Honest about authoring-tool ramp. Adapt as needed at submission.

answers.question: Why are you interested in this role?
answers.answer: I'm drawn to this role because it treats learning as a strategic lever, not just a deliverable. With a master's in Applied Organizational Psychology, I approach instructional design the same way I'd approach research—diagnose the real capability gap before designing anything, partner with subject-matter experts on content, and measure whether the learning actually changed behavior. The chance to build evidence-based leadership development and organizational-effectiveness programs is exactly the work I love most. I've spent the last few years running needs assessments, designing learning materials, and facilitating training for 600+ participants, and I want to bring that diagnose-design-measure mindset to a place where strong programs can travel across an entire institution and genuinely improve how people work and grow.

answers.question: Why UF / UF Human Resources?
answers.answer: What excites me about UF Human Resources is the scale and the mission—you're designing learning for faculty and staff across an entire university, where one well-built program can shape thousands of people's experience. That's the kind of systems-level impact I find energizing. I'm also drawn to the team's evidence-based approach to leadership development and organizational effectiveness; it mirrors how I was trained to work, using needs assessments and feedback loops rather than guessing. I'll be candid that I'd be relocating to Gainesville for this role, and I'm genuinely glad to—being on the ground with the team I'm designing for matters to me. A university HR function feels like the right place to do meaningful, lasting learning work, and I'd be proud to contribute to it.

answers.question: Describe your instructional design / learning program experience.
answers.answer: My experience spans the full design cycle minus heavy authoring production. At InGenius Prep I run needs assessments with 25+ stakeholders to pinpoint learning gaps, then design the guides, workflows, and job aids that close them—work that cut onboarding time by 75%—and I facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants. At NextGen Healthcare I designed and delivered internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review; at Vestas I designed an Inclusive Leadership workshop that scaled to 23 locations. I'll be honest: my strength is design, facilitation, and evaluation rather than deep Articulate Storyline or Rise production. I'm comfortable in Canva and Canvas LMS and ramp on new tools quickly, so I'm confident I can build authoring fluency fast while contributing strong design thinking from day one.
